title: Unfinished placeholder fragments in final section (incomplete arguments)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Summary
The closing portion of `solution.tex` contains literal unfinished fragments that are not typographical but represent abandoned, incomplete arguments (violating gap-free reasoning).

## Locations / fragments
In *Proof of the Theorem* and `lem:residual`:
- "u N_c^{-1}=u N_c ... u^{-1}n_m ∈ N_c^{-1}·**?**"
- "n_m^{-1}**?**"
- "N_c ⊇ **?** — this need not hold in general"
- "γ_{m'}(γ_c(F))-part" (ill-defined object)
- "[ · ]" used as a stand-in
- `lem:residual` ends with "which is automatic and not yet the conclusion" (no QED).

## Required action
Delete the abandoned fragments and the explicitly-circular passages. Note that removing them exposes the substantive hole tracked separately (see the crux issue): the easy inclusion N_c ⊆ γ_c, Lemma `lem:gen`, Lemma `lem:Nc-step`, and the tower (T) are correct and should be kept as a clean lemma; the deduction γ_c ⊆ N_c must then be supplied.
